what_it_does: TypeScript declaration file for the Badge component. Declares a BadgeProps interface extending React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement> with optional tone (a union of 'gold' | 'success' | 'warning' | 'error' | 'comm'), dot (a leading status dot), and as (polymorphic element). Declares the Badge function returning JSX.Element with named and default exports.
observations: tone is a closed union mapping to system voices/states: gold (attention), success/warning/error (status), comm (sage relationship); no tone = neutral ink label
dot?: boolean — a leading status dot in the current colour
as?: any — polymorphic 'render as' escape hatch (loosely typed as any)
Extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>; named and default exports
standouts: The tone union ties the component directly to the design system's semantic voice vocabulary (gold / status colours / comm sage), not arbitrary colours
conventions: Co-located .d.ts mirroring the component, same pattern as Avatar.d.ts
Tone values map to the documented system voices/states (consistent with the colour token roles: gold voice, status-*, comm sage)
as?: any departs from strict typing (a common but loose polymorphic-component shortcut)
concerns: as?: any forfeits polymorphic type safety; a generic element type would be stricter
notes: The type contract for Badge: a span-based status/label chip whose tone is a closed union of system voices (gold/success/warning/error/comm) with an optional leading dot and a polymorphic as.
questions: Why is 'comm' included but not the other zone voices (source/package/review) — is the badge intentionally limited to attention+status+relationship?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — a declaration file that accurately describes the Badge component's typed surface and ties tone to the system's semantic voices.
apparent_themes: Semantic voice vocabulary (tone maps to system meaning, not raw colour)
intention_signals: To expose Badge's props as a precise contract where colour is chosen by semantic tone (a system voice/state) rather than by raw value, keeping the component single-sourced to the token roles.
contribution: Contributes the typed public interface for Badge — the closed tone union mapping to system voices/states, the dot flag, and the polymorphic as — that a fused system would keep as Badge's contract.
summary_for_embedding: TypeScript declarations for the Badge component: a BadgeProps interface with a semantic tone union (gold/success/warning/error/comm), a dot flag, and a polymorphic as, extending span HTML attributes.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a type contract for a status/label badge whose colour is chosen by semantic system tone rather than raw value, keeping it tied to the design-system voices.
